name: HPV16_integration_hotspots
description: HPV16 integration in cervical cancer disrupts E1/E2 in ~70% of cases (preferential breakpoint cluster nt 1500-3500, peak at the E1/E2 boundary nt ~2700-3100), eliminating E2-mediated repression of the early promoter and stabilizing E6/E7 mRNA. Host integration sites are non-random and enriched at common fragile sites (FRA3B/FHIT, FRA8C/MYC, FRA16D/WWOX), super-enhancer regions, and active transcription units. Recurrent host targets include MYC (chr8q24), PIM1, hTERT, KLF12, LRP1B, and the FHIT locus. Integrations form 'looped concatemers' with focal copy number gain of the integrated cassette plus flanking host DNA. Split-read and discordant-pair detection across the L1/LCR boundary is the workhorse for breakpoint calling.
expected_copy_count: highly variable, 1 to >100 integrated copies per cervical-cancer cell
